page: Microsoft Azure D64als_v7 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the Dalsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with 32 physical cores and 64 vCPUs operating at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The instance is configured with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, but it is capable of utilizing Premium Storage.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier single-core and multi-core capabilities. The AMD EPYC processor achieves top-tier status in single-core stress-ng performance and PassMark CPU testing, alongside strong multi-core performance. Memory bandwidth is robust, registering strong read and write speeds for both small and large blocks. Single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression benchmarks place this instance in the top tier, while database operations and Redis SET operations show strong to top-tier throughput.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D64als_v7 offers high compute density and high clock speeds for CPU-bound tasks, though its low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core represents a tradeoff for memory-intensive applications. The lack of local storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which may impact I/O-heavy workloads depending on the attached disk configuration. This instance is suited for compute-heavy workloads, batch processing, web serving, and database operations that do not require massive memory footprints.
description: Microsoft Azure D64als_v7 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server instance from the Dalsv7 family, powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 processor. It features 64 vCPUs (32 physical cores) running at 4.5 GHz, paired with 128.0 GB of RAM, establishing a 2.0 GB per vCPU memory ratio. The instance lacks local storage and integrated GPUs but supports Premium Storage. In benchmark evaluations, the D64als_v7 delivers top-tier single-core performance and strong multi-core throughput, highlighted by high scores in PassMark CPU tests, database operations, and single-threaded compression tasks. Its high clock speed and compute density make it highly cost-effective for CPU-bound applications, though the low memory-to-core ratio is a key tradeoff. This server is best suited for compute-intensive workloads, web serving, and database operations.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D64als_v7 is a compute-optimized instance featuring 64 vCPUs on an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor at 4.5 GHz, 128 GB of RAM, and top-tier single-core benchmark performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D64als_v7 is a 64-vCPU compute-optimized instance with an AMD EPYC 9V45 CPU and 128 GB RAM, designed for CPU-bound workloads.
tagline: A high-frequency, compute-dense virtual server featuring sixty-four vCPUs and a dedicated AMD EPYC processor for intensive processing workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with 64 vCPUs running at 4.5 GHz
128.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU
Top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong memory bandwidth
Premium Storage capable with no local storage bundled
Optimized for compute-heavy tasks, web serving, and database operations
